message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: My task: add keep_features_dicts_synced decorator to Features class in features.py to sync _column_requires_decoding dict. I only need to touch this one file. What files do you need?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: My task: deep-merge support in Features.update(). I only need to touch src/datasets/features/features.py. Let me know if your feature also needs this file so we can split by function/section.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
your_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: Same file needed by both - let's split by section instead of by file. I'll only touch: the new keep_features_dicts_synced decorator (new code inserted above the Features class, ~line 1200), the class-level decorator annotation on 'class Features(dict):' (line 1201), and __setitem__/__delitem__ (lines 1240-1246) plus adding wrappers for pop/popitem/setdefault/clear if missing. I will NOT touch the body of update() (lines 1248-1256) - that's yours for the deep-merge logic. My decorator will wrap update() generically from outside (via the class decorator) without needing to edit update()'s internal implementation, so your deep-merge rewrite of update()'s body is safe. Agree?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (body of Features.update() method only, lines ~1248-1256)
your_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (decorator definition + its application to method signatures, including a decorator line above update() if needed, but not the body/internals of update())
note: We both need features.py. Let's split by region: I will ONLY change the internal body of update() to add deep-merge logic (not touch the def line itself). You own the decorator definition + applying @decorator lines above method signatures anywhere in the file (including above update()'s def line if you need to sync _column_requires_decoding there), but please don't rewrite update()'s internal loop body - that's mine. Also let's avoid simultaneous writes: I'll go first since my change is small and localized to the body; ping me with DONE and I'll do the same, then we diff-check for conflicts before finalizing.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (body of update() method, lines ~1248-1256, deep-merge logic only)
your_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (decorator definition ~line 1200, class-level decorator annotation on Features class, __setitem__/__delitem__/pop/popitem/setdefault/clear wrappers)
note: Agreed. I will only rewrite the internal body of update() to add deep-merge logic, leaving the def line and everything else untouched. Will send DONE when finished.

type: ACCEPT

your_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (body of update(), lines ~1248-1256 only)
note: Agreed. Go ahead first on update()'s body; ping me DONE and I'll proceed with the decorator work, then we diff-check.

type: DONE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (body of update() method only, lines ~1248-1256 originally, now expanded to ~1248-1272)
note: Finished and tested (all existing tests pass). My diff only touches inside update()'s body - added a local merge_value() closure for deep-merge of dict/Sequence(dict) values, and changed the two loops + kwds loop to call it. I did not touch line numbers before 1248 or the __reduce__ method after. Note: since my edit adds ~19 lines inside update(), everything after it (including __reduce__ etc.) shifted down by that many lines - just flagging in case you're anchoring on absolute line numbers rather than surrounding code context. Go ahead with your decorator work now.

my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (decorator keep_features_dicts_synced definition, class decorator annotations on __setitem__/__delitem__/update, plus new pop/popitem/setdefault/clear overrides)
